response.justification: Bogdan brings genuinely relevant experience: current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, prior high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of requests per second), and strong TypeScript. However, the role demands strong Python, which is entirely absent from his resume — his systems work was in DLang, a niche stack. Evidence of staff-level technical strategy and cross-functional stakeholder leadership is thin; most roles read as senior IC product delivery. The AI-native mindset fit is strong enough to warrant consideration, but the Python gap and limited demonstrated strategy scope keep this from a clear yes.
strengths: Current AI-native experience building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure
Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Strong TypeScript/Node plus deep testing and software-craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)
concerns: No Python listed anywhere — a must-have skill for this role
Limited evidence of directing technical strategy or managing stakeholders at staff scope
High-throughput systems experience is from 2018-2020 and in DLang, not a mainstream stack
